| MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | # Create React App Firebase Starter Template | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/cra-template-firebase-starter.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/cra-template-firebase-starter) | MCP:GjPW1Z | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dy/cra-template-firebase-starter.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/cra-template-firebase-starter) | MCP:GjPW1Z | [![License MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-orange.svg?style=flat)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter/main/LICENSE) | MCP:GjPW1Z | [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-Welcome-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter/pulls) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-20232A?style=for-the-badge&logo=react&logoColor=61DAFB) | MCP:GjPW1Z | ![NPM](https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-CB3837?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&logoColor=white) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Make sure you ⭐️ this [`repository`](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter) if you find it helpful or interesting :) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z |

Go to your [`Firebase Console`](https://console.firebase.google.com/) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 2. Click on `Add project`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | - Follow steps and create project. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 3. Click on web icon. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | - Register web app. | MCP:GjPW1Z | - You will get details for setting up `Firebase SDK`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 4. Add the config details in your `.env` file (available in root of your project). | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### SetUp SignIn with google | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 1. In your project console, click on `Authentication` and `Get Started`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 2. In `Sign-in method`, click on `Google`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 3. Toggle `Enable` and click on `Save`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### To create a new screen | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 1. Create a new component in `src/screens`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 2. In your `src/config/routes.ts`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ```js | MCP:GjPW1Z | { | MCP:GjPW1Z | path: "/your-route", | MCP:GjPW1Z | component: YourScreenComponentName, | MCP:GjPW1Z | name: "Screen Name For Reference", | MCP:GjPW1Z | protected: false, // if user needs to be authenticated to access this screen | MCP:GjPW1Z | } | MCP:GjPW1Z | ``` | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### To Deploy on GitHub Pages | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 1. Add your domain (your-github-username.github.io) to the OAuth redirect domains list in the `Firebase console -> Auth section -> Sign in method tab`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 2. update the `homepage` field in your `package.json` to the domain you are hosting on. (By default it is: `http://your-github-username.github.io/your-repo-name`) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 3. (Optional) Test production build locally: Update the `homepage` field to `/` and run: | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ```bash | MCP:GjPW1Z | npm i -g serve # install serve globally via npm | MCP:GjPW1Z | npm run build-and-serve | MCP:GjPW1Z | ``` | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | 4. Deploy to GitHub Pages run: | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ```bash | MCP:GjPW1Z | npm run deploy | MCP:GjPW1Z | ``` | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ## ✨ Featues | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### 1. Routing | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Routing setup using `react-router-dom`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | - With easily implementable private routes (routes only authenticated users can use). | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | - Easily add new screens. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### 2. Authentication | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Authentication implemented implemented | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | SignIn with Google implemented using `firebase` and `react-firebase-hooks`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### 3. Firebase Configured | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Firebase configured with `GoogleAuthProvider` implemented. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### 4. Material UI | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Login Screen UI built using `@mui/material`. | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ### 5. Deploy on Github Pages | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Deploying on GitHub pages powered by `gh-pages` and [`spa-github-pages`](https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ## 🔧 File Structure | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ``` | MCP:GjPW1Z | ├── src | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ ├── components | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── auth | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ | ├── AuthChecker.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ | ├── AuthContainer.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ | ├── Logout.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── utils | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ | ├── Center.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ ├── config | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── firebase.config.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── firebase.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── routes.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ ├── screens | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── Login.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | │ │ ├── Home.js | MCP:GjPW1Z | ``` | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | ## 📝 Authors | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | - Hussain Pettiwala ([@pettiboy](https://github.com/pettiboy)) | MCP:GjPW1Z | | MCP:GjPW1Z | Special thanks to this well written [`blog`](https://grischuk.de/how-to-create-custom-create-react-app-cra-templates) and this [`repo`](https://github.com/alexandr-g/cra-template-typescript-redux) by [alexandr-g](https://github.com/alexandr-g).

data.content: Now I have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive summary. Let me check the 404.html file to understand the SPA routing solution used for GitHub Pages.

content.text: | MCP:GjPW1Z | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: ddefb08db770d9b40bf9c1c17a997854049f7011)

| MCP:GjPW1Z | <!DOCTYPE html>
| MCP:GjPW1Z | <html>
| MCP:GjPW1Z |   <head>
| MCP:GjPW1Z |     <meta charset="utf-8" />
| MCP:GjPW1Z |     <title>Single Page Apps for GitHub Pages</title>
| MCP:GjPW1Z |     <script type="text/javascript">
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // Single Page Apps for GitHub Pages
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // MIT License
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // This script takes the current url and converts the path and query
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // string into just a query string, and then redirects the browser
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // to the new url with only a query string and hash fragment,
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // e.g. https://www.foo.tld/one/two?a=b&c=d#qwe, becomes
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // https://www.foo.tld/?/one/two&a=b~and~c=d#qwe
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // Note: this 404.html file must be at least 512 bytes for it to work
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // with Internet Explorer (it is currently > 512 bytes)
| MCP:GjPW1Z | 
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // If you're creating a Project Pages site and NOT using a custom domain,
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // then set pathSegmentsToKeep to 1 (enterprise users may need to set it to > 1).
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // This way the code will only replace the route part of the path, and not
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // the real directory in which the app resides, for example:
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // https://username.github.io/repo-name/one/two?a=b&c=d#qwe becomes
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // https://username.github.io/repo-name/?/one/two&a=b~and~c=d#qwe
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       // Otherwise, leave pathSegmentsToKeep as 0.
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       var pathSegmentsToKeep = 1;
| MCP:GjPW1Z | 
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       var l = window.location;
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       l.replace(
| MCP:GjPW1Z |         l.protocol +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           "//" +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           l.hostname +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           (l.port ? ":" + l.port : "") +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           l.pathname
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .split("/")
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .slice(0, 1 + pathSegmentsToKeep)
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .join("/") +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           "/?/" +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           l.pathname
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .slice(1)
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .split("/")
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .slice(pathSegmentsToKeep)
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .join("/")
| MCP:GjPW1Z |             .replace(/&/g, "~and~") +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           (l.search ? "&" + l.search.slice(1).replace(/&/g, "~and~") : "") +
| MCP:GjPW1Z |           l.hash
| MCP:GjPW1Z |       );
| MCP:GjPW1Z |     </script>
| MCP:GjPW1Z |   </head>
| MCP:GjPW1Z |   <body></body>
| MCP:GjPW1Z | </html>

data.content:
Here's a comprehensive summary of how to install, configure, and deploy this React Firebase starter to GitHub Pages:

---

## 🚀 Installation

### Option A: Use the npm template (recommended)
```bash
npx create-react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter
```
or with Yarn:
```bash
yarn create react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter
```

### Option B: Clone the repo directly
```bash
git clone https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T02_R02.git your-project-name
cd your-project-name
npm install
```

---

## ⚙️ Firebase Configuration

### 1. Create a Firebase project
1. Go to the [Firebase Console](https://console.firebase.google.com/).
2. Click **Add project** and follow the steps to create your project.
3. Click the **Web icon** to register a web app.
4. You'll receive your Firebase SDK config credentials.

### 2. Create a `.env` file in the project root
The template reads Firebase config from environment variables. Create a `.env` file at the root of your project with these variables (values from your Firebase console):

```
REACT_APP_FirebaseApiKey=your-api-key
REACT_APP_FirebaseAuthDomain=your-project-id.firebaseapp.com
REACT_APP_FirebaseProjectId=your-project-id
REACT_APP_FirebaseStorageBucket=your-project-id.appspot.com
REACT_APP_FirebaseMessagingSenderId=your-sender-id
REACT_APP_FirebaseAppId=your-app-id
```

> **Note:** The `.env` file is already in `.gitignore`, so your secrets won't be committed.

### 3. Enable Google Sign-In
1. In the Firebase Console, go to **Authentication** → **Get Started**.
2. Under **Sign-in method**, click on **Google**.
3. Toggle **Enable** and click **Save**.

The Firebase initialization is handled in `src/config/firebase.js` using the config from `src/config/firebase.config.js`, which reads the environment variables above. The app uses `firebase` (v9 modular SDK) with `GoogleAuthProvider`.

---

## 🛣️ Adding Routes / Screens

The app uses `react-router-dom` v6. To add a new screen:

1. Create a new component in `src/screens/`.
2. Add a route entry in `src/config/routes.js`:
   ```js
   {
     path: "/your-route",
     component: YourScreenComponentName,
     name: "Screen Name For Reference",
     protected: false,  // set to true if the user must be authenticated
   }
   ```

The template comes with two routes out of the box:
- `""` (root) → `Home` screen (**protected**, requires authentication)
- `/login` → `Login` screen (public)

---

## 📦 Deploying to GitHub Pages

The template is pre-configured with the [`gh-pages`](https://github.com/tschaub/gh-pages) package and includes a `404.html` file from [`spa-github-pages`](https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages) to enable client-side routing on GitHub Pages.

### Steps:

1. **Add your GitHub Pages domain to Firebase OAuth redirect domains.**
   - In the Firebase Console → **Authentication** → **Settings** → **Authorized domains**, add your domain (e.g., `your-username.github.io`).

2. **Update the `homepage` field in `package.json`.**
   - Change it to your GitHub Pages URL:
     ```json
     "homepage": "https://your-github-username.github.io/your-repo-name"
     ```
   - The generated project (from the template) sets `homepage` to `"."` by default, which works for relative paths.

3. **(Optional) Test the production build locally:**
   ```bash
   npm i -g serve       # install serve globally if needed
   npm run build-and-serve
   ```

4. **Deploy:**
   ```bash
   npm run deploy
   ```
   This runs the `predeploy` script (`npm run build`) automatically, then uses `gh-pages -d build` to push the contents of the `build` folder to a `gh-pages` branch on your repository.

5. **Configure GitHub repository settings:**
   - Go to your GitHub repo → **Settings** → **Pages**.
   - Set the source to the `gh-pages` branch.
   - Your app will be live at `https://your-github-username.github.io/your-repo-name`.

---

### Key Architecture Notes

| Feature | Implementation |
|---|---|
| **Framework** | React 18 with Create React App |
| **Routing** | `react-router-dom` v6 with protected/private route support |
| **Authentication** | Firebase Auth with Google Sign-In (`firebase` v9 + `react-firebase-hooks`) |
| **UI** | Material UI (MUI) v5 |
| **Deployment** | `gh-pages` + SPA-friendly `404.html` redirect hack |
| **SPA routing on GH Pages** | `public/404.html` uses the [spa-github-pages](https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages) technique with `pathSegmentsToKeep = 1` |
